1421	\begin{theorem}
1422	
1423	
1424	[Generic flatness, geometric form]
1425	  \label{thm:generic_flatness}
1426	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
1427	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
1428	  % NOTE (re-signed): the Lean signature `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
1429	  % previously took `F : X.Modules` with NO coherence hypothesis, under which the
1430	  % statement is FALSE (a non-coherent module sheaf need not become flat on any
1431	  % non-empty open). The decl (not in archon-protected.yaml) was re-signed to carry
1432	  % the coherence encoding below; the Lean file is the source of truth for the exact
1433	  % binders and the full flatness conclusion.
1434	  %
1435	  % Mathlib has no single `IsCoherent` predicate at this pin; coherence over a
1436	  % locally noetherian base is encoded as the conjunction of two existing
1437	  % SheafOfModules predicates on `F`:
1438	  %     [F.IsQuasicoherent]   (SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent,
1439	  %                            Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Quasicoherent)
1440	  %     [F.IsFiniteType]      (SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType,
1441	  %                            Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Generators)
1442	  %
1443	  % LEAN SIGNATURE HEADER (now landed in the .lean file):
1444	  %   theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
1445	  %       (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules)
1446	  %       [F.IsQuasicoherent] [F.IsFiniteType] :
1447	  %       ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧ (the fibrewise flatness conclusion,
1448	  %       quantified over affine opens U ≤ V of S and affine opens W ≤ p⁻¹U of X, with
1449	  %       Γ(F,W) given its Γ(S,U)-module structure via p.appLE — verbatim form in the
1450	  %       .lean file, the source of truth).
1451	
1452	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
1453	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
1454	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1455	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
1456	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
1457	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
1458	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
1459	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
1460	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
1461	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
1462	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
1463	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
1464	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
1465	
1466	  The coherence hypothesis on \(\F\) is essential: a non-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module
1467	  need not become flat over any non-empty open of \(S\).
1468	\end{theorem}
1469	
1470	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
1471	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
1472	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
1473	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
1474	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
1475	% by restricting to a non-empty
1476	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
1477	\begin{proof}
1478	  
1479	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\), where \(A\) is a
1480	  noetherian domain (\(S\) integral and locally noetherian). We assemble the witness
1481	  \(V\) from the algebraic form on a finite affine cover.
1482	
1483	  \emph{Step 1 — finite affine cover above \(U_0\).} Since \(p\) is locally of finite
1484	  type it is in particular quasi-compact, so the open subscheme \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\)
1485	  is quasi-compact; choose a finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of
1486	  \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each
1487	  ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\) is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.
1488	
1489	  \emph{Step 2 — read off a finite module on each patch.} On each \(W_j\), quasi-coherence
1490	  of \(\F\) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the
1491	  section module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and the finite-type hypothesis makes \(M_j\)
1492	  a finite \(B_j\)-module. Through the scalar tower \(A \to B_j \to M_j\), \(M_j\) is
1493	  the data fed to the algebraic form.
1494	
1495	  \emph{Step 3 — apply the algebraic form and take the common basic open.}
1496	  \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} applied to \((A, B_j, M_j)\) yields \(f_j \in A\),
1497	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
1498	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and
1499	  \(V := D(f) \subseteq \Spec A \subseteq S\), a non-empty basic open; over \(V\) every
1500	  \((M_j)_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
1501	
1502	  \emph{Step 4 — freeness implies flatness.} On each patch the localized section module
1503	  is free, hence flat over \(A_f\); the fibrewise flatness conclusion then follows from
1504	  these local freeness statements by the criterion that a module flat at every maximal
1505	  ideal is flat. This produces the required \(V\) with \(\F|_{X_V}\) flat over \(\OO_V\).
1506	\end{proof}

1508	\section{Mathlib status}
1509	\label{sec:flatstrat_mathlib_status}
1510	
1511	Mathlib (master \texttt{b80f227}) provides \texttt{Module.Flat}
1512	(\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic}), the module-theoretic notion of
1513	flatness applicable affine-locally to the section modules of \(\F\), together
1514	with the dévissage and Noether-normalisation infrastructure that the algebraic
1515	generic-flatness argument consumes
1516	(\texttt{IsNoetherianRing.induction\_on\_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime},
1517	\texttt{Module.FinitePresentation.exists\_free\_localizedModule\_powers},
1518	\texttt{exists\_finite\_inj\_algHom\_of\_fg}).  It does \emph{not} yet contain
1519	the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness (a finite module over
1520	\(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) becomes free after inverting a single element), nor the
1521	geometric statement \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
1522	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness}) packaging the algebraic form
1523	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}) over a noetherian integral base.  These
1524	are introduced in
1525	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}.

869	/-- **L5b.1 — annihilator extraction for the torsion module**
870	(`lem:gf_torsion_annihilator`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 0`,
871	and `T` a finite *torsion* module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exists a
872	non-zero `F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`. Since `P_d` is a domain, a non-zero-divisor
873	annihilator (provided by `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`) is the
874	same thing as a non-zero one. -/
875	theorem gf_torsion_annihilator
876	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (d : ℕ)
877	    (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
878	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
879	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
880	    ∃ F : MvPolynomial (Fin d) A, F ≠ 0 ∧
881	      F ∈ Module.annihilator (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T := by
882	  obtain ⟨F, hFann, hFnzd⟩ := Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors htors
883	  refine ⟨F, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hFnzd, ?_⟩
884	  rw [← Submodule.annihilator_top]
885	  exact hFann

889	The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
890	change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
891	unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
892	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation

894	`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
895	(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a

897	bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
898	a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/

1006	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <

1037	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
1038	      (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A),

1091	theorem mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar
1092	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ)
1093	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R)
1094	    (hp : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).leadingCoeff) :
1095	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1096	        (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom :
1097	          MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →+* (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})).Finite := by
1098	  letI algI : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) :=
1099	    ((Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {p})).comp
1100	      (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ).toRingHom).toAlgebra
1101	  have hc : IsUnit ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :=
1102	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit
1103	  have hcP :
1104	      IsUnit (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)) :=
1105	    hc.map Polynomial.C
1106	  have hmonic : (Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1107	      * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p).Monic := by
1108	    rw [Polynomial.Monic, Polynomial.leadingCoeff_C_mul_of_isUnit hc]
1109	    exact Units.inv_mul_of_eq hp.unit_spec
1110	  have hspan : Ideal.span {Polynomial.C ((hp.unit⁻¹ : (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)ˣ) :
1111	        MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) * MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}
1112	      = Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p} :=
1113	    Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit hcP (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p)

1116	    rw [← hspan]; exact hmonic.finite_quotient

1119	          MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R →+* Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R))
1120	        (Ideal.span {p}) := by

1122	  let ψ : (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p})
1123	      ≃+* (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) ⧸ Ideal.span {MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n p}) :=

1127	      ψ (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)
1128	          (MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) R ⧸ Ideal.span {p}) s)
1129	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin n) R)

1146	/-- Pull back an `R`-module structure along an additive equivalence `e : M ≃+ N`, defining
1147	`r • y := e (r • e.symm y)`. With this structure `e` is `R`-linear; used in the torsion
1148	reindex to transport the reindexed module structure from the `P`-localisation of the torsion
1149	module to the goal's `A`-localisation. -/
1150	@[reducible] def pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1151	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] (e : M ≃+ N) : Module R N where
1152	  smul r y := e (r • e.symm y)
1153	  one_smul y := by change e (1 • e.symm y) = y; rw [one_smul, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1154	  mul_smul r s y := by
1155	    change e ((r * s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm (e (s • e.symm y)))
1156	    rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, mul_smul]
1157	  smul_zero r := by change e (r • e.symm 0) = 0; rw [map_zero, smul_zero, map_zero]
1158	  smul_add r x y := by
1159	    change e (r • e.symm (x + y)) = e (r • e.symm x) + e (r • e.symm y)
1160	    rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add]
1161	  add_smul r s y := by
1162	    change e ((r + s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm y) + e (s • e.symm y)
1163	    rw [add_smul, map_add]
1164	  zero_smul y := by change e (0 • e.symm y) = 0; rw [zero_smul, map_zero]
1165	
1166	/-- Module-finiteness transports across the pulled-back structure of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1167	if `M` is a finite `R`-module then so is `N` under the pulled-back action. -/
1168	theorem finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1169	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] (e : M ≃+ N) :
1170	    @Module.Finite R N _ _ (pullbackModuleAddEquiv e) := by
1171	  letI : Module R N := pullbackModuleAddEquiv e
1172	  refine Module.Finite.equiv (M := M) (N := N)
1173	    { toFun := e, map_add' := map_add e, invFun := e.symm,
1174	      left_inv := e.left_inv, right_inv := e.right_inv,
1175	      map_smul' := fun r x => ?_ }
1176	  simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1177	  change e (r • x) = e (r • e.symm (e x))
1178	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply]

1180	/-- A scalar tower transports across the pulled-back structures of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1181	if `e : M ≃+ N` and `M` carries compatible `Ag`- and `R`-actions (`IsScalarTower Ag R M`), then
1182	the pulled-back `Ag`- and `R`-actions on `N` form a scalar tower as well. -/
1183	theorem pullback_isScalarTower {Ag R M N : Type*} [CommSemiring Ag] [Semiring R] [Algebra Ag R]
1184	    [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module Ag M] [IsScalarTower Ag R M]
1185	    (e : M ≃+ N) :
1186	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1187	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1188	    IsScalarTower Ag R N := by
1189	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1190	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1191	  refine ⟨fun a b x => ?_⟩
1192	  change e ((a • b) • e.symm x) = e (a • e.symm (e (b • e.symm x)))
1193	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, smul_assoc]

1196	compatible with given `R`-algebra structures. If `ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂` satisfies
1197	`ψ ∘ algebraMap R B₁ = algebraMap R B₂`, `B₂` is module-finite over `R`, and `M` is
1198	module-finite over `B₁` (with the scalar tower `R → B₁ → M`), then `M` is module-finite
1199	over `R`. -/
1200	theorem finite_of_quotientRingEquiv
1201	    {R B₁ B₂ M : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B₁] [CommRing B₂] [AddCommGroup M]
1202	    [Algebra R B₁] [Algebra R B₂] [Module B₁ M] [Module R M] [IsScalarTower R B₁ M]
1203	    (ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂)
1204	    (hψ : ∀ r : R, ψ (algebraMap R B₁ r) = algebraMap R B₂ r)
1205	    (hB₂ : Module.Finite R B₂) (hM : Module.Finite B₁ M) :
1206	    Module.Finite R M := by
1207	  haveI := hB₂
1208	  haveI := hM
1209	  have hsymm : ∀ r : R, ψ.symm (algebraMap R B₂ r) = algebraMap R B₁ r := by
1210	    intro r; rw [← hψ r, ψ.symm_apply_apply]
1211	  haveI : Module.Finite R B₁ :=
1212	    Module.Finite.equiv (AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := ψ.symm) hsymm).toLinearEquiv
1213	  exact Module.Finite.trans B₁ M

1217	restriction of scalars to `R'` localises `M` at `S` over `R'`. -/
1218	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars
1219	    {R' R M M' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [CommRing R] [Algebra R' R]
1220	    [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R' M] [Module R M] [Module R' M'] [Module R M']
1221	    [IsScalarTower R' R M] [IsScalarTower R' R M']
1222	    (S : Submonoid R') (f : M →ₗ[R] M')
1223	    [IsLocalizedModule (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f] :
1224	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R') := by
1225	  apply IsLocalizedModule.mk
1226	  · intro s
1227	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1228	    have hmem : algebraMap R' R (s : R') ∈ S.map (algebraMap R' R) := ⟨s, s.2, rfl⟩
1229	    have hu := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f ⟨algebraMap R' R (s : R'), hmem⟩
1230	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff] at hu
1231	    have hfun : ⇑((algebraMap R' (Module.End R' M')) (s : R'))

1233	      funext x
1234	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
1235	    rw [hfun]
1236	    exact hu

1239	    obtain ⟨s, hsS, hs⟩ := sm.2
1240	    refine ⟨⟨t, ⟨s, hsS⟩⟩, ?_⟩
1241	    change (s : R') • y = (f.restrictScalars R') t
1242	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_apply, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') y, hs]

1248	    refine ⟨⟨s, hsS⟩, ?_⟩

1250	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₁,
1251	        ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₂, hs]

1262	a finite torsion module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `g ≠ 0` in `A` and
1263	`m' < d` such that, after inverting `g`, the localisation `T_g` is module-finite
1264	over `A_g[X_1,…,X_{m'}]` (one may take `m' = d - 1`). -/
1265	theorem gf_torsion_reindex
1266	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1267	    (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
1268	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1269	    [Module A T] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1270	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
1271	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
1272	      (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
1273	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
1274	      (_ : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
1275	              (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
1276	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
1277	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
1278	        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) := by
1279	  -- Reshape `d = m + 1` (uses `hd : 0 < d`); the distinguished eliminated variable is `X₀`.
1280	  obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hd.ne'
1281	  -- L5b.1 (`gf_torsion_annihilator`): a non-zero annihilator `F` of the torsion module `T`.
1282	  obtain ⟨F, hF0, hFann⟩ := gf_torsion_annihilator A (m + 1) T htors
1283	  -- L5b.2 (`gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`): a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a
1284	  -- non-zero `g ∈ A` making `e F` monic in `X₀` up to a unit after inverting `g`.
1285	  obtain ⟨g, hg0, e, hunit⟩ := gf_nagata_monic_lastVar A m F hF0
1286	  -- L5b.3 (`mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar`): the coefficient subring
1287	  -- `A_g[X₁,…,X_m] = MvPolynomial (Fin m) A_g` has `A_g[X₀,…,X_m]/(map (e F))` module-finite
1288	  -- over it; this is the source of the dimension drop `m' = m < m + 1`.

1290	    (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F)) hunit
1291	  classical
1292	  -- ASSEMBLY. Strategy: work with the *`P`-localisation* `Tg' := LocalizedModule (C(powers g)) T`
1293	  -- of `T` (it inherits the full `P_g`-module API, finiteness, and quotient structure for free),
1294	  -- prove `Tg'` module-finite over `R := MvPolynomial (Fin m) A_g`, and finally transport the
1295	  -- module structures + finiteness to the goal's `T_g := LocalizedModule (powers g) T` (the
1296	  -- `A`-localisation) along the canonical `A_g`-linear equivalence `T_g ≃ₗ[A_g] Tg'`.
